science_p5_water_cycle.core_concept: Water changes state based on heat transfer. Evaporation happens at any temperature; boiling happens at 100 degrees Celsius.

science_p5_plant_transport.core_concept: Plants transport water/minerals from roots to leaves, and food from leaves to all parts of the plant. Do NOT use secondary school terms like xylem or phloem.

science_p4_light_shadows.core_concept: Shadows are formed when light, which travels in a straight line, is blocked by an opaque or translucent object.

math_p1_number_bonds.core_concept: Establish part-whole relationships using physical counting blocks or pictorial representations before introducing formal + and - operators.

math_p4_whole_numbers.core_concept: Solve word problems using the Concrete-Pictorial-Abstract (CPA) approach. Students must draw bar models to represent unknown quantities.

math_p6_ratio.core_concept: Compare quantities using units. Identify if the heuristic is 'Total Unchanged', 'Difference Unchanged', or 'One Quantity Unchanged'.

english_upper_synthesis_reported.core_concept: Transform direct speech to reported speech by shifting tenses backwards one step and updating time/place markers. Punctuation must be perfectly strict.

chinese_upper_composition.core_concept: Structure the narrative clearly. The story must have a moral lesson or reflection (感想) at the end. Use appropriate idioms (成语) where natural.

chinese_sg_localization.core_concept: Strictly adhere to Singapore MOE local vocabulary. Penalize standard Mainland/Taiwanese terms. When the student uses a non-SG term, immediately correct with the local equivalent and explain: 'In Singapore, we say [SG term], not [non-SG term].'

science_p4_heat.core_concept: Objects gain heat from a hotter source and lose heat to a cooler sink. Use ONLY 'gained heat from' and 'lost heat to' phrasing. Never say 'got hotter' — this is a 0-mark trap in PSLE.

science_p4_states_of_matter.core_concept: Matter changes state when it gains or loses enough heat to reach its melting/boiling point. Use strict MOE phrasing: 'reached its boiling point' NOT 'turned into steam'. This is a critical 0-mark PSLE trap.

forbidden_words: iron is magnetic because it is metal
science_p3_magnets.core_concept: NOT all metals are magnetic — only iron, steel, cobalt, and nickel. Copper, aluminium, gold, and silver are metals but NOT magnetic materials. Say 'magnetic material' not just 'metal'. The word 'stick to' must be replaced with 'attract'.

math_heuristics_p1_p2.core_concept: P1-P2 students must use ONLY concrete counting, number bonds, and pictorial methods. Algebra is STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. Do not use any algebraic notation, variables, or equation-solving terminology.

math_heuristics_p3_p4.core_concept: P3-P4 students MUST use Bar Models (Part-Whole or Comparison). The model method is mandatory for multi-step word problems. Do not introduce algebra. Always say 'draw the model' before solving.

math_heuristics_p5.core_concept: P5 introduces the Assumption Method: 'Suppose all items are Type A, then the excess/shortage is...' This is the ONLY acceptable method for guess-and-check problems. Do NOT use simultaneous equations or algebraic substitution.

math_heuristics_p6.core_concept: P6 introduces basic linear algebra: 'Let the unknown be x'. Simple substitution and expression simplification are allowed. Bar models should still be used alongside algebra. Do NOT use secondary school methods like simultaneous equations or quadratics.
